questions.prompt: Why is it recommended to start in a simulator when learning FPV?
options: Because flying in a simulator is harder than the real thing, so you push yourself
Because it provides cheap, safe, unlimited practice; if you crash you don't break parts or hurt anyone
Because flying in a simulator is legally mandatory
Because real drones won't power on without a simulator
questions.explanation: A simulator is free, you can't hurt anyone, and you can repeat endlessly. Having your first crashes here removes both the cost and the stress.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT a popular FPV simulator?
options: Liftoff
Velocidrone
DRL (Drone Racing League) Sim
Betaflight Configurator
questions.explanation: Liftoff, Velocidrone, Uncrashed, and DRL Sim are popular FPV flight simulators. Betaflight Configurator is not a simulator but the configuration software where you connect the drone's flight controller (FC) over USB and set it up.

questions.prompt: What is the most practical way to use your own FPV radio in a simulator?
options: Connecting the radio over USB and selecting 'USB Joystick' mode
Not connecting the radio at all and flying with the mouse
Removing the radio's battery
Placing your phone on top of the radio
questions.explanation: Connecting the radio with a USB cable and selecting 'USB Joystick' mode is the most practical way to present it directly as a joystick without any extra hardware.

questions.prompt: Why is not calibrating the sticks in a simulator a problem?
options: The simulator won't open at all
The graphics look broken
The drone may keep drifting without you releasing the stick, and you build a bad habit from the start
The radio's battery drains faster
questions.explanation: Bad calibration causes the drone to drift even with the sticks centered. This makes you build an incorrect control habit from the very beginning.

questions.prompt: How does the stick control learned in a simulator transfer to a real drone?
options: It doesn't transfer at all; the simulator teaches a completely different skill
The same stick movements and reflexes work on a real drone too; muscle memory forms through repetition
It only works on racing drones, not for freestyle
It only works if you buy the same brand of drone
questions.explanation: The stick feel and muscle memory you build in a simulator transfer directly to a real drone. That's why even professional pilots use simulators for training.
